auth_block_oauth:
AUTHENTICATE via OAuth — handled by your MCP client (e.g. Claude) in the browser.
There are NO password/token tools: just call a data tool, and if you are not signed in
the client runs the sign-in/consent flow for you. NEVER ask the user for a password or a
token, and do NOT pass `bearer_token` — the client attaches the token automatically.
auth_block_legacy:
AUTHENTICATE before any data tool. The user needs a FlexReport account + a JWT:
- Existing user: call get_token(username=<email>, password=<password>) -> access_token.
- New user (no account): call register_user(email, password); the backend emails a
  confirmation link/token — have the user click the link (or paste the token to
  confirm_registration); then call get_token.
Pass the access_token as the `bearer_token` argument on every data tool
(list_realtime_events, get_latest_report, generate_report, generate_research_report,
onboard_symbol). On HTTP 401 the token expired — call
get_token again and retry. Do NOT ask the user to paste tokens into config files.
instructions:
FlexReport Finance — a real-time equity research platform. Instant retrieval of
real-time, analyst-grade research for over 2,900 companies, delivered on demand as tools.

{auth_block}

=== ROUTING: MATCH THE USER'S INTENT TO THE RIGHT TOOL ===
Decide intent FIRST, then pick the tool. Reports come from three tools — choosing the
right one matters:

1) STANDARD report for a company  ->  get_latest_report(symbols=[...])   [THE DEFAULT]
   Intent: a plain, not-heavily-customized ask for "the report / research / analysis /
   writeup / latest research / the PDF" on one or more tickers
   (e.g. "get me the latest research on SNOW", "pull the report for AAPL and MSFT").
   This returns the pre-built, cached report INSTANTLY. Prefer it first, every time the
   intent is simply "give me the research on <ticker>".

2) OPEN-ENDED or THEMATIC question  ->  generate_research_report(query=...)
   Intent: an exploratory or thesis-style question — broad questions about a ticker, or
   a market-wide theme NOT tied to one company's standard report
   (e.g. "Are large caps driving earnings season?", "What's the bull/bear case on NVDA?",
   "high-growth semis with rising estimates", "how are AI names holding up this quarter?").
   This composes analyst-grade research to answer the question directly. Route here
   whenever the request is a QUESTION rather than a request for an existing report.
   BEFORE calling this tool, ASK the user how they want the result delivered —
   `delivery="dashboard"` (the default) or `delivery="email"`. This is a long-running
   job (~10-12 minutes), so email is often preferable: the user can walk away and the
   finished report is sent to them rather than waiting on a live poll. Confirm the
   choice first, then pass the chosen `delivery` value.

3) CUSTOM / bespoke report  ->  generate_report(ticker, overrides=...)   [LAST RESORT]
   Use this ONLY when EITHER:
     (a) get_latest_report has NO cached report for the ticker (it came back in
         `missing`), so there is nothing to return, OR
     (b) the user EXPLICITLY wants a customized report — specific line items, ratios,
         filing frequency, institutional-ownership cuts, or other overrides the cached
         report cannot cover.
   It builds a brand-new report on the fly and is SLOW/ASYNC. NEVER reach for it on an
   ordinary "get me the research" request — that is what get_latest_report is for.

Rule of thumb: existing report wanted -> get_latest_report; a QUESTION to research ->
generate_research_report; only-if-customized-or-missing -> generate_report.

EXPLORE FIRST, THEN DEEP-DIVE: explore_data_catalogue(query=...) is the quick,
exploratory pass — it runs queries against the data platform and returns raw result
sets for INTERACTIVE CHARTS AND TABLES, so the user can get a sense of what data exists
and iterate on questions. Route here when the intent is to poke at / scope the data
rather than get a finished writeup (e.g. "what data do you have on semi margins?",
"show me revenue growth across large-cap software"). Once the user is satisfied with what
they've explored, hand the settled question to generate_research_report for the full,
professional deep-dive. Always delivered to the dashboard; rate-limited 20/hour.

POWER TOOLS — curate smarter insights beyond a single company's report:
- Stock screening: screen_stocks(...) — surface names by financial growth, sector,
  sub-industry, market cap, analyst ratings, institutional ownership, country, and
  price performance.
- Portfolio optimization: optimize_portfolio_default(...) for a fast, synchronous
  risk-optimized portfolio; optimize_portfolio(...) for the slower LLM-curated variant.
- Stock picks across strategies: get_stock_picks(...) — the current rebalance holdings
  the backend's multiple strategies are positioned in.
- Live market events: list_realtime_events(...) — real-time earnings/EPS/8-K/rating
  moves from the backend's event cache.

NO AUTH NEEDED: list_report_options, list_tickers, list_sub_industries,
get_company_snapshot, get_stock_picks, get_task_status. Use list_report_options(...)
to discover valid parameter values instead of guessing.
